title: Power, Cooling, Specialized Workstations, and Ergonomic Fit
description: A design lab needs stable workstations for video, CAD, virtualization, and remote meetings, but measured load, power quality, acoustics, thermals, access needs, and budget disagree with a generic build list. Learners cumulatively produce power-thermal-workload fit model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Match wattage headroom, rails, connectors, efficiency, form factor, input, protections, GPU transients, and vendor guidance to measured requirements.
Distinguish surge protection, standby and line-interactive UPS behavior, runtime, load, battery health, grounding, and graceful shutdown needs.
Design intake, exhaust, pressure, filtration, cable clearance, ambient, acoustic, sensor, and maintenance controls around observed heat.
